name: Three Pot
description: This algorithm allows you to run what are effectively DSP “plug-ins”, loaded from the MicroSD card. It was designed particularly to support programs written for the Spin Semiconductor 126 FV-1 chip. This popular IC has ended up in numerous products including some Eurorack modules (e.g. the FX Aid and the Timiszoara). Its popularity and the relative ease of developing for it has led to a large number of (generally open source) DSP programs being available, and it is this that the Three Pot algorithm was designed to leverage. The name “Three Pot” comes from the FV-1 hardware design – all it has are stereo inputs and outputs, and connections for three pots for user controls. Although the focus is on converted FV-1 programs, the conversion works by translating the programs to C++ and then compiling them for the disting NT – so you can if you like actually write C++ directly and use this algorithm to run it on the module.
short_description: Runs DSP code from the SD card
gui_description: The UI for this algorithm is currently unique on the disting NT in that it not only provides a custom display, but it also changes the way the knobs work. The display shows the program name, pot labels, and description. The three pots on the module control the three pot parameters directly. Additionally, the right encoder changes the program.

specifications.name: Code memory
specifications.description: The amount of code memory to assign to the algorithm (in multiples of 512 bytes).

parameters.name: Pre/post gain
parameters.description: Sets the gain adjustment applied before the program’s DSP code.

parameters.name: Mix style
parameters.description: Chooses the dry/wet mix style: ‘None’, ‘Wet/dry’, or ‘Independent’.

parameters.name: Mix
parameters.description: Sets the mix if the style is ‘Wet/dry’.

parameters.name: Dry gain
parameters.description: Sets the dry gain if the mix style is ‘Independent’.

parameters.name: Wet gain
parameters.description: Sets the wet gain if the mix style is ‘Independent’.

parameters.name: Sample rate
parameters.description: The sample rate (in kHz) at which to run the program.
